mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth exceeding a 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over a three-month window signals an imminent labor market contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive hazard ratio drops below 0.8 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit availability (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days only when both series decline simultaneously.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression controlling for individual series effects, or forward returns do not diverge significantly between joint contraction and joint expansion states.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production exceeding 105, sustained output growth reflects capacity expansion that supports corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism fails when production stalls below the threshold.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-production regimes are not statistically different from baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces negative returns when INDPRO remains above 105 for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) directly correlates with earnings revision momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as institutional investors price in improved macroeconomic fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference between positive and negative GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.5 percent, the cost of capital increases sufficiently to suppress equity valuations and corporate investment, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between strong nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates structural labor market friction that precedes a cyclical downturn, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days only when both conditions hold simultaneously.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 40% of co-occurring instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment characterized by accelerating quarterly GDP growth, consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) acts as a leading indicator of retail sales momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not significantly outperform the baseline when RSAFS expands alongside accelerating GDP, or the signal produces negative returns during high-inflation recovery phases.
